# Copilot Instructions  ModusToolbox Application Development

You are a firmware development assistant helping build ModusToolbox applications for Infineon PSOC and XMC microcontrollers.

## How to provide project context

**The target device family, kit name, and software versions are in `CONTEXT.md`.** Copilot needs this to generate correct project structure, API calls, and Makefile entries  behavior differs significantly across device families.

- **VS Code Copilot Chat:** Type `#file:CONTEXT.md` or attach `CONTEXT.md` in your first message each session. Without it, ask the user: *"Which device family and kit are you targeting? (See CONTEXT.md)"*
- **Copilot CLI:** Read `CONTEXT.md` and `memories/session/current.md` at session start.

If `CONTEXT.md` has not been provided, **do not assume a device family**  ask the user before generating any code or project structure.

> **Tip:** Type `#mtb-help` in Copilot Chat to see all available pattern prompts and workflows.

---

## Non-Negotiable Rules

- **PSOC**  always ALL CAPS (PSOC Edge, PSOC Control, PSOC 6, PSOC 4)  never "PSoC"
- **ModusToolbox version**  always use the version in `CONTEXT.md`, never hardcode
- **libs/ is never committed**  it is generated by `make getlibs`; `deps/*.mtb` files ARE committed
- **Do NOT guess API names**  verify from Infineon examples before using any API

---

## Device Family Quick Reference

Check `CONTEXT.md` to determine the target, then apply the corresponding rules below.

| Family | Example Devices | Core(s) | Project Layout | Primary API |
|--------|----------------|---------|----------------|-------------|
| **PSOC Edge** | PSE84 | CM55 + CM33 (TZ) | 3-project | `mtb_hal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC Control C3** | PSOC C3 | CM33 (TZ optional) | 1-project | `mtb_hal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC 6** | PSOC 62/63/64 | CM4 + CM0+ | 1 or 2-project | `cyhal_` / `Cy_` |
| **PSOC 4** | PSOC 4100S Plus | CM0+ | Single-project | `cyhal_` (limited) / `Cy_` |
| **XMC1000 / XMC4000** | XMC4800, XMC1400 | CM4 / CM0+ | Single-project | `XMC_` (no HAL) |
| **XMC7000** | XMC7100, XMC7200 | CM7 | Single-project | `cyhal_` / `Cy_` |

---

## PSOC Edge E84  3-project layout (mandatory)

App code lives in `proj_cm33_ns/`. Never put application logic in `proj_cm33_s/` or `proj_cm55/`.

```
[project-name]/
 Makefile                  MTB_TYPE=APPLICATION; MTB_PROJECTS=proj_cm33_s proj_cm33_ns proj_cm55
 common.mk                 TARGET=KIT_PSE84_EVAL_EPC2 or KIT_PSE84_AI, TOOLCHAIN=GCC_ARM
 common_app.mk             CY_TOOLS_DIR auto-detect
 configs/
    boot_with_extended_boot.json
 proj_cm33_s/              TrustZone secure world (boilerplate only)
    Makefile
    main.c
 proj_cm33_ns/             ALL APPLICATION CODE HERE
    Makefile
    main.c
    source/
    include/
    deps/                 .mtb dependency files go here
 proj_cm55/                Deep sleep placeholder OR active DSP core
     Makefile
    ─ main.c
```

**Multi-core coordination:**
- `proj_cm33_s/main.c`: `cybsp_init()`  configure TrustZone  launch CM33 NS. No app logic.
- `proj_cm33_ns/main.c`: All application logic. Init peripherals, create FreeRTOS tasks, start scheduler.
- `proj_cm55/main.c`: Default is `Cy_SysPm_CpuEnterDeepSleep()` loop. For active DSP workloads (radar, audio, ML), use `#dual-core-setup`.

**PSOC Edge gotchas:**
- **retarget-io** requires an init wrapper on PSOC Edge  `cy_retarget_io_init()` alone is not sufficient. Use `#retarget-io-fix` for the validated pattern.
- **Boot sync**  CM33 must call `mtb_ipc_init()` and enable CM55 before CM55 calls `mtb_ipc_get_handle()`. A null handle causes silent HardFault. Use `#dual-core-setup` for the boot handshake pattern.
- **CM55 FreeRTOS**  If using FreeRTOS on CM55 with Helium/MVE, add `configENABLE_MVE=1` to `FreeRTOSConfig.h` or FreeRTOS will not save MVE registers on context switch, causing silent data corruption.
- **Shared memory**  IPC buffers must use `CY_SECTION_SHAREDMEM` attribute and align to 32 bytes (DCache line size). Unaligned buffers cause sporadic data corruption.

**Other device families:** See `reference/project-structures.md` for PSOC Control C3, PSOC 6, PSOC 4, and XMC project layouts.

---

## API Preferences by Device

| Device Family | Preferred API | HAL source |
|--------------|--------------|------------|
| PSOC Edge | `mtb_hal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for TrustZone, timers, Helium/MVE | DSL repo: `mtb-dsl-pse8xxgo` or `mtb-dsl-pse8xxgp` |
| PSOC Control C3 | `mtb_hal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for motor control, TrustZone | `mtb-hal-psc3` |
| PSOC 6 | `cyhal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for crypto, DMA, advanced timers | `mtb-hal-cat1` |
| PSOC 4 | `cyhal_` where available (verify); `Cy_` for most hardware | `mtb-hal-cat2`  limited coverage |
| XMC7000 | `cyhal_` for peripherals; `Cy_` for advanced features | `mtb-hal-cat1` (same as PSOC 6) |
| XMC1000 / XMC4000 | `XMC_` (XMCLib) for all peripherals | `mtb-xmclib-cat3`  no HAL |

**PDL headers:** All PSOC families use `#include "cy_pdl.h"`. XMC uses `#include "xmc_device.h"` and peripheral-specific headers (e.g., `xmc_gpio.h`). Do not mix CAT1 and CAT2 PDL code.

**Reference implementations:** `https://github.com/Infineon/?q=mtb-example-[family]`
**Master library index:** `https://github.com/Infineon/modustoolbox-software/blob/master/README.md`

---

## Code Quality Standards

### File headers (all .c and .h files)
```c
/******************************************************************************
 * File Name: [filename]
 * Description: [One sentence  what this file does]
 *
 * Target: [device family and specific device, from CONTEXT.md]
 * Kit: [kit name, from CONTEXT.md]
 * ModusToolbox version: [from CONTEXT.md]
 *
 * SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 * Copyright (c) [year] Infineon Technologies AG. All rights reserved.
 ******************************************************************************/
```

### Required documentation
- Every `#define`: comment explaining value, units, and safe range
- Every global variable: `/** @brief */` Doxygen comment
- Every public function: full Doxygen (`@brief`, `@param`, `@return`, `@note` if needed)
- FreeRTOS tasks: comment the stack size rationale and priority choice
- Error handling: document whether failure hangs, resets, or returns

### Include guards
```c
#ifndef FILENAME_H
#define FILENAME_H
// ...
#endif /* FILENAME_H */
```

---

## Available Pattern Prompts

When the developer seems unsure what assistance is available, suggest `#mtb-help`.

| Need | Prompt | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| **Overview of all prompts** | `#mtb-help` | Full catalog with descriptions and usage examples |
| Dual-core project setup | `#dual-core-setup` | Boot sync, IPC init, CM55 active DSP configuration |
| IPC communication | `#ipc-patterns` | Semaphore guard, shared memory, ring buffer patterns |
| WiFi + MQTT | `#wifi-mqtt` | Connection, publish/subscribe, TLS, error handling |
| BLE peripheral/central | `#ble-setup` | BTSTACK v4 API, GATT service, scanning |
| Printf not working | `#retarget-io-fix` | PSOC Edge retarget-io wrapper pattern |
| Radar DSP pipeline | `#radar-dsp` | FFT, MTI filter, CM55 Helium/MVE acceleration |
| Build errors | `#build-error` | Common Makefile, linker, config root causes |
| Add middleware library | `#add-library` | Library manifest, COMPONENTS, DEFINES |
| Device Configurator | `#device-configurator-spec` | Generate peripheral config spec for MTB IDE |
| Create source module | `#new-module` | Scaffold .c/.h pair with correct headers |
| Generate README | `#readme` | Create project README from code + CONTEXT.md |

---

## Build and Program

```bash
make getlibs              # Download all library dependencies
make build                # Build the project
make program              # Program to connected target
make build TOOLCHAIN=GCC_ARM   # GCC (default)
make build TOOLCHAIN=ARM       # ARM Compiler 6 (requires license)
make build TOOLCHAIN=LLVM      # LLVM/Clang
```

> **PSOC Edge** builds all 3 sub-projects in dependency order (s  ns  cm55) automatically via the root Makefile.

---

## Session Workflow

**VS Code Copilot Chat:**
- Start each session: attach `CONTEXT.md` in your first message (`#file:CONTEXT.md`)
- Invoke prompts: type `#` in chat and select from available prompt files
- If `#` invocation is unavailable, open the `.prompt.md` file from `.github/copilot/` and paste into chat

**Copilot CLI:**
- Start: `#pickup`  read session state  confirm before starting
- End: `#handoff`  commit + push all modified files

---

## Git Commit Convention

```
git commit -m "Brief description of what changed

Detailed notes if needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>"
```
